notes.content: The Cell Biology major provides students with a comprehensive understanding of the cell, the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms. To understand living organisms, the biologist must understand the cell. Hence, cell biology lies at the core of the biological sciences. Students taking this major gain a solid foundation in biological principles. The major emphasizes how cellular organization and function contribute to the development, maintenance, and reproduction of adult organisms. The major illustrates the ways in which principles derived from the physical sciences, genetics, biochemistry, molecular biology, and physiology are integrated in the study of living cells and emphasizes the experimental nature of the study of cell biology.
notes.contentHtml:
The Cell Biology major provides students with a comprehensive understanding of the cell, the basic structural and functional unit of all living organisms.


To understand living organisms, the biologist must understand the cell. Hence, cell biology lies at the core of the biological sciences. Students taking this major gain a solid foundation in biological principles. The major emphasizes how cellular organization and function contribute to the development, maintenance, and reproduction of adult organisms. The major illustrates the ways in which principles derived from the physical sciences, genetics, biochemistry, molecular biology, and physiology are integrated in the study of living cells and emphasizes the experimental nature of the study of cell biology.

notes.content: The major provides an excellent background for students wishing to enter postgraduate and professional programs in biological, health sciences or veterinary sciences; for students pursuing careers involving teaching or research in the biological sciences; for students interested in careers in the biotechnological or pharmaceutical industries; or for students interested in careers related to the administrative, legal, or commercial aspects of biomedical science.
notes.contentHtml: The major provides an excellent background for students wishing to enter postgraduate and professional programs in biological, health sciences or veterinary sciences; for students pursuing careers involving teaching or research in the biological sciences; for students interested in careers in the biotechnological or pharmaceutical industries; or for students interested in careers related to the administrative, legal, or commercial aspects of biomedical science.

notes.content: This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission. Important Information: You must complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series: ● Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C ● Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C ● Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission) ● Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C ● Physics 7A/B/C Visit Department agreements as needed Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions ( Find my advisor ) Note: Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination: AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog . Quick reference charts for AP and IB .
notes.contentHtml:
This is a competitive major and is subject to the Selective Major Review process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.

 

Important Information:


You must complete the courses below and maintain a 2.50 GPA in each series:

●     Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C

●     Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C

●     Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B


Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission)


●     Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C

●     Physics 7A/B/C


Visit Department agreements as needed


Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (Find my advisor)


Note:

Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:

AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis General Catalog. Quick reference charts for AP and IB.

notes.content: At the time of TAG submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.
notes.contentHtml: At the time of TAG submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum 3.20 cumulative GPA and at least a 2.50 GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.

notes.content: Group 2, Group 3 and Group 4: Other course combinations may be acceptable. Please refer to the major and an advisor.
